mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls reduces labor market slack, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs compress equity valuations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or t-test p-value > 0.05 for return differential.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in consumer credit balances signals supply chain bottlenecks coupled with debt-fueled demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days due to margin compression.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns do not significantly decline when INDPRO falls and RSAFS rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for consumer spending recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE > 6.0% and HOUST rises, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized, corporate earnings accelerate and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with a falling unemployment rate signals an overheating economy that triggers inflation fears, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE, or t-test p-value > 0.05 for return differential.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate cash flows.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in industrial production growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 230, inflation expectations anchor higher, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL > 230, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
